text: Yes, but conditionally on difficulty. On a hard-enough puzzle the granularity cost-benefit replicates and is real - coarse is reproducibly cheaper than none with non-overlapping CIs - which answers both q-0004 (the ratios survive at the hard end) and q-0005 (the signal is conditional on difficulty), and clears the Phase-1 gate. (Evidence: e-0004, puzzle_002 n=3: coarse/none=0.55x, $0.72 gap.)
